perturbation.original_text: The Reseller intends to market the Portals to companies and various businesses and individuals ("BUYERS") who may include corporations, small businesses, religious organizations, network marketing, franchise, business opportunities, chains, charities, organizations and other companies that would have a use for individual and multiple portals.
perturbation.changed_text: The Reseller intends to market the Portals to companies and various businesses and individuals ("CUSTOMERS") who may include corporations, small businesses, religious organizations, network marketing, franchise, business opportunities, chains, charities, organizations and other companies that would have a use for individual and multiple portals.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'BUYERS' to 'CUSTOMERS', we introduce an inconsistency. Throughout the agreement, 'Buyers' is consistently used to refer to those who purchase portals from the reseller, however we have now started using 'CUSTOMER' instead, without defining it, leading to confusion. For example, in the Compensation Schedule, the term 'Buyers' is still used exclusively, creating uncertainty about whether the compensation structure applies to 'Customers' as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: COMPENSATION SCHEDULE This Compensation Schedule is attached hereto and made a part thereof that certain Reseller Agreement of even date: Reseller shall be entitled to receive compensation in the following manner: COMPENSATION: BSP REWARDS PROGRAM: MediaNet shall, upon collection, remit to Member Provider _1_% of the net Rewards earned by Members through and provided directly by it, and _1_% of the net Member Rewards received and collected that is earned through Merchants, Companies, Organizations, Groups and individuals that have been contracted through Member Provider. BRAND-A-PORT OR BSP PORTAL SALES: MediaNet shall remit to Member Provider 20% of the price of each Portal sold directly by it. BSP MONTHLY HOSTING FEES: In addition, MediaNet shall remit to Member Provider an amount equal to 10% of the hosting fees paid by Buyers who purchase portals or host websites with MediaNet as a direct result of the activities of Member Provider, whether those activities are sold through the portal or independent of it. PAYMENT OF COMPENSATION (SUBJECT TO RECEIPT OF GOOD, VALID FUNDS) PORTAL SALES: MediaNet shall remit all sums due to Member Provider within 30 days of receipt of cleared funds. MONTHLY HOSTING FEES: In addition, MediaNet shall remit hosting fees to Member Provider in monthly installments on or before the 15th business day of each month. All sums received by MediaNet through the last business day of the preceding month shall be included in the calculation of the amount to be remitted. BSP REWARDS MEMBER PURCHASES VOLUME: Amounts due for purchases of products or services shall be calculated as of funds received by the last day of each month and remitted on or before the 15th day of the following month. RECORDS MediaNet shall maintain books and records and shall provide for online confirmation of sales and payments. MediaNet shall permit Member Provider or its designees reasonable access during normal business hours and, upon request, to verify funds and payments due pursuant to this Agreement. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have caused this Agreement to be executed effective as of the day and year last set forth below.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'BUYERS' to 'CUSTOMERS' introduces a misaligned terminology, creating an in-text contradiction. The Compensation Schedule uses the term 'Buyers' to define who is purchasing the portal, directly linking it to the remittance calculation. By changing 'Buyers' to 'CUSTOMERS' in the earlier definition, it introduces uncertainty as to whether the 'CUSTOMERS' purchasing portals are the same as the 'Buyers' referred to in the Compensation Schedule. This undermines the clarity of the payment obligations and who they apply to, fulfilling the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition. Therefore, there's a strong contradiction that could cause confusion and potentially legal disputes.

perturbation.original_text: Under the plan, resellers market and promote MediaNet Group Technologies's software product range to their existing customer base and the general Internet community. By referring a customer to the MediaNet Group Technologies web site, the reseller receives a commission for customer's purchase of the MediaNet Group Technologies Brand-A-Port software.
perturbation.changed_text: Under the plan, marketers market and promote MediaNet Group Technologies's software product range to their existing customer base and the general Internet community. By referring a customer to the MediaNet Group Technologies web site, the marketer receives a commission for customer's purchase of the MediaNet Group Technologies Brand-A-Port software.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'resellers' with 'marketers' in this section of the Reselling Schedule, without defining 'marketers', the contract creates ambiguity. This contradicts Section 1 and 2 that outline that the agreement is for 'Resellers', leading to questions as to who is entitled to the commissions and what obligations marketers have under the broader agreement. This creates a contradiction as the 'Reselling Schedule' applies to the the 'Reseller Agreement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. LICENSE Reseller is hereunder licensed to market MediaNet Group Technologies' Brand-A-Port portals ("PORTALS") and to resell MediaNet Group Technologies products and services for compensation in accordance with the annexed "RESELLING SCHEDULE." Under the terms and conditions as outlined in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines the agreement as being with 'resellers'. The changed text replaces 'resellers' with 'marketers' in the Reselling Schedule. Section 1 explicitly states the 'LICENSE Reseller is hereunder licensed' and refers to the 'RESELLING SCHEDULE'. By changing 'resellers' to 'marketers' without defining 'marketers' or clarifying their relationship to the Reseller Agreement, a contradiction is created. It becomes unclear whether the Reselling Schedule now applies to marketers, and if so, what their obligations are under the broader Reseller Agreement. This ambiguity creates a direct conflict with Section 1, which clearly outlines the terms and conditions for 'Resellers' only.

perturbation.original_text: Resellers must send us an E-mail with the company name, contact and title that you wish to register with the Company. Said contact must have the authority to make the decision relative to the Brand-A-Port and/or BSP programs.
perturbation.changed_text: Distributors must send us an E-mail with the company name, contact and title that you wish to register with the Company. Said contact must have the authority to make the decision relative to the Brand-A-Port and/or BSP programs.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Resellers' with 'Distributors' in PROCEDURE FOR REGISTERING PROSPECTIVE CLIENTS, MEMBER PROVIDERS/ MERCHANTS/MERCHANT MEMBER PROVIDERS, without defining 'Distributors', the contract creates ambiguity. 'Resellers' is the defined term in the overall agreement for the other contracting part, so it isn't clear now who should follow PROCEDURE FOR REGISTERING PROSPECTIVE CLIENTS, MEMBER PROVIDERS/ MERCHANTS/MERCHANT MEMBER PROVIDERS.
perturbation.location: PROCEDURE FOR REGISTERING PROSPECTIVE CLIENTS, MEMBER PROVIDERS/ MERCHANTS/MERCHANT MEMBER PROVIDERS
perturbation.contradicted_text: RESELLER AGREEMENT THIS AGREEMENT (the "AGREEMENT") entered into as of the 19 day of March, 2004 (the "EFFECTIVE DATE") provides the terms and conditions under which MediaNet Group Technologies, Inc., a Nevada corporation having an address at 5100 W. Copans Road Suite 710 Margate, FL 33063 USA ("MEDIANET GROUP TECHNOLOGIES"), authorizes International Direct Response, Inc., a Pennsylvania Corporation, having an address at 1125 Lancaster Avenue, Berwyn, PA 19312("RESELLER") to be its true and lawful representative and agent for the purpose of reselling licenses of the MediaNet Group Technologies Portals ("PORTALS") and other MediaNet Group Technologies products and services in accordance with the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement. This Agreement, together with the other agreements and schedules referenced in it, contains the complete terms and conditions between the parties. MEDIANET GROUP TECHNOLOGIES AND RESELLER AGREE AS FOLLOWS:
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly defines the contracting party as a 'RESELLER'. By changing 'Resellers' to 'Distributors' in the PROCEDURE FOR REGISTERING PROSPECTIVE CLIENTS, MEMBER PROVIDERS/ MERCHANTS/MERCHANT MEMBER PROVIDERS without defining 'Distributors', the modified text creates a strong contradiction with the 'RESELLER AGREEMENT' section. The change makes it unclear who the registration procedure applies to, because the agreement explicitly defines the other party as a 'RESELLER', not a 'Distributor', fulfilling the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
